perturbation.original_text: (mm) "Transaction Documents" means, collectively, this Agreement, the Exclusive Supplier Operating Agreement, the Warrant, the Indemnification Agreement and the Amendment.
perturbation.changed_text: (mm) "Transaction Documents" means, collectively, this Agreement, the Exclusive Supplier Operating Agreement, the Indemnification Agreement and the Amendment. The Warrant shall not be considered part of these documents.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'the Warrant' from the definition of 'Transaction Documents', the agreement introduces uncertainty. Many clauses refer to 'Transaction Documents' as a whole. Now, it's unclear whether clauses pertaining to all Transaction Documents also apply to the Warrant. This creates confusion, especially regarding rights and obligations tied to the Warrant, such as registration rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Authority; Approvals. The Company has full corporate power and authority to execute and deliver each of the Transaction Documents to which it is a party and to consummate the transactions contemplated thereby. The execution and delivery of each of the Transaction Documents to which it is a party and the consummation by the Company of the transactions contemplated thereby, including, without limitation, the issuance of the Purchased Shares, the Warrant and the underlying Warrant Shares, have been duly and validly approved by all necessary corporate action of the Company, and no other corporate and no shareholder proceedings on the part of the Company are necessary to approve such Transaction Documents or to consummate the transactions contemplated thereby. The Board of Directors has taken all actions so that the restrictions contained in Section 203 of the Delaware General Corporation Law, as amended (the "DGCL"), applicable to a "business combination" (as defined in Section 203 of the DGCL) do not and will not apply to the execution, delivery or performance of any Transaction Document and the transactions contemplated hereby and thereby. Each of the Transaction Documents to which it is a party has been duly and validly executed and delivered by the Company and (assuming due execution and delivery by Farids and EA, as applicable) constitutes a valid and binding obligation of the Company, enforceable against the Company in accordance with its terms, except as enforcement may be limited by general principles of equity whether applied in a court of law or a court of equity and by bankruptcy, insolvency and similar laws affecting creditors' rights and remedies generally (collectively, the "Enforceability Exceptions").
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'Warrant' from the definition of 'Transaction Documents'. The contradicted text explicitly states that the Company has the authority to execute and deliver 'each of the Transaction Documents', including the 'Warrant'. By excluding the 'Warrant' from the definition of 'Transaction Documents', it creates a direct contradiction as to whether the clause regarding authority and approvals extends to the 'Warrant'. This introduces uncertainty about the enforceability of the warrant, which violates the structural flaws definition of in-text contradictions.

perturbation.original_text: (y) "Permitted Transfer" means (i) any Transfer to one or more entities that are, directly or indirectly, wholly owned by Farids or any Family Member of TF, (ii) any Transfer to one or more entities that are members of the Farids Group and (iii) Transfer made following a Change in Control of Farids or EA or made pursuant to a Change in Control that constitutes a sale of the Company as a whole; provided, with respect to each of clause (i)-(iii), so long as the transferee (other than a transferee that already is party to this Agreement) agrees to be subject to the terms of this Agreement (subject to any limitation on the assignment of rights by such Person to the transferee in connection with such Transfer) by executing and delivering a joinder agreement, substantially in the form of Exhibit A hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: (y) "Permitted Transfer" means (i) any Transfer to one or more entities that are, directly or indirectly, wholly owned by Farids or any Family Member of TF, (ii) any Transfer to one or more entities that are members of the Farids Group and (iii) Transfer made following a Change in Control of Farids or EA or made pursuant to a Change in Control that constitutes a sale of the Company as a whole; provided, with respect to each of clause (i)-(iii).
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'so long as the transferee (other than a transferee that already is party to this Agreement) agrees to be subject to the terms of this Agreement (subject to any limitation on the assignment of rights by such Person to the transferee in connection with such Transfer) by executing and delivering a joinder agreement, substantially in the form of Exhibit A hereto.' there is uncertainty, as to wheter a transferee needs to sign the joinder agreement to make the transfer permited, or that requirement is not in place.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) The Farids Group acknowledges and agrees that any Transfer of the limited liability company interests, partnership interests, shares or other similar equity interests in any member of the Farids Group or a parent entity of such member will be deemed to constitute a Transfer of Purchased Shares, and any proposed Transfer of all or any portion of any such interests in any member of the Farids Group or a parent entity of such member shall be subject to compliance with the terms of this Agreement as such terms apply to the Farids Group.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that for a transfer to be a 'Permitted Transfer,' the transferee must agree to be subject to the agreement by executing a joinder agreement. The changed text removes this requirement, creating uncertainty about whether a joinder agreement is necessary. The contradicted text states that any transfer of interests within the Farids Group is subject to the terms of the agreement. Without the joinder agreement requirement, it's unclear how the transferee becomes bound by the agreement's terms, directly conflicting with the requirement that any such transfer 'shall be subject to compliance with the terms of this Agreement'. The removal of the joinder requirement creates a direct conflict with the stated requirement that all transfers within the Farid group are subject to compliance, introducing a strong in-text contradiction because it makes the mechanism for that compliance uncertain, if not impossible.

perturbation.original_text: (a) Until the second anniversary of the Closing Date, the Farids Group agrees not to make any Transfer of all or any portion of the Purchased Shares, except that the Farids Group shall be permitted to make Permitted Transfers.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) Until the second anniversary of the Closing Date, the Farids Group agrees not to make any Transfer of all or any portion of the Purchased Shares, except that the Farids Group shall be permitted to make Transfers approved by the Company's CEO.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Permitted Transfers' to 'Transfers approved by the Company's CEO', we introduce a contradiction. The term 'Permitted Transfer' is defined in Section 1.1, and the current version in Section 5.1(a) respects this definition. By changing it, the text creates two separate rules for what constitutes an allowable transfer, leading to uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (y) "Permitted Transfer" means (i) any Transfer to one or more entities that are, directly or indirectly, wholly owned by Farids or any Family Member of TF, (ii) any Transfer to one or more entities that are members of the Farids Group and (iii) Transfer made following a Change in Control of Farids or EA or made pursuant to a Change in Control that constitutes a sale of the Company as a whole; provided, with respect to each of clause (i)-(iii), so long as the transferee (other than a transferee that already is party to this Agreement) agrees to be subject to the terms of this Agreement (subject to any limitation on the assignment of rights by such Person to the transferee in connection with such Transfer) by executing and delivering a joinder agreement, substantially in the form of Exhibit A hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows 'Permitted Transfers' as defined in Section 1.1(y). The changed text replaces 'Permitted Transfers' with 'Transfers approved by the Company's CEO', directly conflicting with the defined 'Permitted Transfers' in Section 1.1(y). This introduces two different and potentially conflicting standards for allowable transfers, creating a clear in-text contradiction and rendering the section ambiguous.
